title: The Optimal Paper Moebius Band
why_relevant: This is the central paper: it proves the sharp sqrt(3) lower bound for smooth embedded paper Möbius bands and identifies the triangular limiting object. Its T-pattern/bend-foliation arguments are the most directly adaptable proof technology for the squeeze-map and clean-triangulation formulation.

title: On Nearly Optimal Paper Moebius Bands
why_relevant: This gives an effective quantitative stability version of the optimal-paper-Möbius theorem. The estimates controlling bands with aspect ratio close to sqrt(3) are useful for turning an infimum statement into a robust contradiction or convergence-to-triangle argument.

title: The Optimal Paper Moebius Band and Finite Tensegrity Calculations
why_relevant: Although superseded by the later Annals proof, this research note develops a finite tensegrity/edge-inequality viewpoint. That is especially close in spirit to the problem’s squeeze maps, where boundary edges are shortened and transverse edges lengthened.

title: Inverting a Cylinder Through Isometric Immersions and Isometric Embeddings
why_relevant: This is the original Halpern-Weaver paper containing the classical pi/2 lower bound, the sqrt(3) upper construction, and the conjecture solved later by Schwartz. Its homotopy and total-curvature methods are foundational for any proof of an optimal aspect-ratio statement.

title: The Optimal Twisted Paper Cylinder
why_relevant: This proves a sharp optimal-aspect-ratio theorem for a closely related quotient surface. It reuses and extends the T-pattern/limiting-folded-polygon machinery from the Möbius-band problem, providing useful analogies for discrete or PL variants.

title: Translation of Michael Sadowsky's Paper "An Elementary Proof for the Existence of a Developable Möbius Band and the Attribution of the Geometric Problem to a Variational Problem"
why_relevant: Sadowsky’s construction is the classical existence mechanism for developable paper Möbius bands. It is useful for the upper-bound direction, i.e. building examples for aspect ratios above the triangular threshold.

title: Translation and Interpretation of Michael Sadowsky's Paper "Theory of Elastically Bendable Inextensible Bands with Applications to the Möbius Band"
why_relevant: This contains Sadowsky’s foundational differential-geometric model of inextensible ribbons and Möbius bands. The discussion of rectifying developables, rulings, and bending energy is relevant to the smooth theory underlying the discrete squeeze-map model.

title: Über ein abwickelbares Möbiusband
why_relevant: Wunderlich’s paper is a key classical source on finite-width developable Möbius bands. It refines Sadowsky’s framework and supplies constructions and geometric constraints that inform the existence side and the developable-surface intuition.

title: Isometric Immersions and Embeddings of a Flat Möbius Strip in Euclidean Spaces
why_relevant: Sabitov gives explicit formulae and geometric analysis for isometric immersions and embeddings of flat Möbius bands. This is useful background for comparing the quotient of the strip by the glide reflection with concrete embedded models.

title: Geometric Realization of Möbius Triangulations
why_relevant: This paper studies when triangulations of the Möbius band can be realized as embedded flat-faced polyhedral surfaces in R^3. Its PL realization techniques are directly relevant to clean triangulations and affine-on-triangles maps.

title: Möbius Bands, Unstretchable Material Sheets and Developable Surfaces
why_relevant: Chen and Fried clarify the distinction between being developable and being an isometric deformation of a prescribed flat strip. That distinction is important when trying to convert PL or smooth developable constructions into valid realizations of the given flat Möbius quotient.

title: Closed Unstretchable Knotless Ribbons and the Wunderlich Functional
why_relevant: This gives a rigorous formulation of closed inextensible ribbons, including nonorientable cases, and carefully handles isometry and local injectivity constraints. It is useful for variational or compactness-based approaches to paper Möbius bands.

title: Construction of Unknotted and Knotted Symmetric Developable Bands
why_relevant: This paper provides explicit constructions of symmetric developable bands, including Möbius bands, using cylinder-scaffold ideas descending from Sadowsky. Such constructions can help prove the realizability side for aspect ratios above the critical value.
